steps.title: Use the parallel-segment similarity
steps.content: Since $ST \parallel QR$, triangle $PST$ is similar to triangle $PQR$. With $RT=2PT$, the full side is $PR=PT+RT=3PT$, so the scale factor from triangle $PQR$ to triangle $PST$ is $\frac{1}{3}$.

steps.title: Use the scale shortcut: find the linear ratio
steps.content:
Since $ST \parallel QR$, triangle $PST \sim$ triangle $PQR$. Given $RT = 2PT$, the full segment is $PR = PT + 2PT = 3PT$, so:
$$\frac{PT}{PR} = \frac{1}{3}$$

steps.title: Apply the ratio to ST
steps.content:
Corresponding sides scale by $\frac{1}{3}$:
$$ST = \frac{1}{3} \times QR = \frac{1}{3} \times 180 = 60$$
The answer is 60.
